name: LUDWIG ANGINA
definition: serious potentially life-threatening necrotizing infection of floor of mouth rapidly involving
dominant_finding: ± extension deep into submandibular + parapharyngeal spaces + pharynx → airway
discriminating_features: loss of fat planes within submandibular space

general: loss of fat planes within submandibular space
ill-defined abscesses in submandibular / sublingual spaces
CT: inflammatory change with small cystic spaces = serosanguinous accumulation in floor of
± extension deep into submandibular + parapharyngeal spaces + pharynx → airway
well-defined fluid collection with rimlike enhancement

clinical.Spread: by direct extension of infection = tooth apices of 2nd / 3rd molar of jaw extend
clinical.Cx: mandibular osteomyelitis, spread of infection into deep fascial spaces of neck,
clinical.Rx: IV antibiotics, aggressive drainage, surgical airway creation
